steps.title: Spot the key demand
steps.content:
The phrase “the degree of this difference varied across the stories” points directly to what we need: stories with noticeably different gap sizes between spoiled and unspoiled ratings.

To best illustrate this variation, pick a pair where the spoiler effect is dramatically different—one story showing a large gap, one showing a smaller gap.

steps.title: Compare gap sizes across the choices
steps.content:
Reading the graph: “Owl Creek Bridge” has a gap of roughly 1.5 points (unspoiled ~5, spoiled ~6.5), while “A Chess Problem” shows a substantially larger gap of roughly 2.5–3 points (unspoiled ~3, spoiled ~5.5–6).

This stark difference in gap sizes directly demonstrates how the spoiler effect varies across stories.

The other options either pair stories with similar gaps (“Blitzed” and “A Chess Problem” both show large effects) or fail to show meaningful contrast.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
